title: Nova Lingua Value Expression
description: Structured AST for Nova Lingua values. Used (in v0.2+) for fields like `examples.args[i]`, `examples.result`, and any other position where a concrete value appears in a function record. Covers thirteen kinds: bool, int, nat, float, string, bytes, unit, list, tuple, record, variant, fn_ref, map (the last added in v0.4 — spec/expressiveness.md phase 2). The bare-string convention currently used in examples.args (e.g. `"double"` to refer to a function) is a v0.1 informal shortcut; v0.2+ will require this structured form in function-record records and rely on bare strings only as JSON-literal string values inside `string`. Well-formedness (type-correct values, record-field uniqueness, map-key uniqueness/ordering, variant tag resolution against a known sum) is the verifier's responsibility.

value.description: Any Nova Lingua value. Discriminated by the 'kind' field.

Signed integer. JSON numbers are used when the value fits within IEEE-754 double precision exactly (|value| < 2^53). Larger magnitudes MUST be encoded as decimal-digit strings, per spec/canonical-serialization.md.
Non-negative integer. Same big-int convention as `int`.
IEEE-754 double-precision floating point. JCS canonicalization does NOT permit NaN, +Infinity, or -Infinity; do not use them here.
Raw bytes, base64-encoded (standard alphabet, padded). Other encodings deferred.

unit.description: The single inhabitant of the `unit` type.

elems.description: Tuple elements in positional order. A zero-element tuple is the `unit` value (use `unit` kind, not a 0-elem `tuple`). A one-element value is just the element itself.

fields.description: Field-value pairs. JSON Schema cannot express 'unique by .name'; the verifier checks. Canonical form sorts fields lexicographically by name.

tag.description: Variant tag, PascalCase per the sum-type convention in type-expression.schema.json.

payload.description: Variant payload. Omit for nullary variants (e.g. `None`, the constructors of an enum).

variant.description: One inhabitant of a sum type. The verifier resolves the tag against the expected sum type.

target.description: Content-address of a function in the commons.

fn_ref.description: A first-class function value, referenced by its commons content-address. Replaces the bare-string-naming-a-function convention used in v0.1 examples (`"double"` etc.).

key.description: Map key. String keys only (v0.4 scope — spec/expressiveness.md phase 2); arbitrary strings, unlike record field names.

entries.description: Key-value entries. JSON Schema cannot express 'unique by .key' or ordering; the verifier enforces both: keys unique, entries sorted by key in Unicode code-point order — the canonical form, so equal maps serialize (and hash) identically.

map.description: A finite map with string keys — an inhabitant of the `Map string a` type constructor (spec/expressiveness.md phase 2). Homogeneous values (verifier enforces); canonical form sorts entries by key.
